summary:
You are given an array of n + 1 integers where every value lies between 1 and n inclusive. By the pigeonhole principle at least one value is repeated. Return the single number that appears more than once.

The challenge is to do this without modifying the array and using only constant extra space. Treating each value as a next pointer turns the array into a linked structure whose cycle entrance is the duplicate.

examples.explanation: The value 2 occurs twice; all other values appear once.
The value 3 is the repeated number.

constraints: 1 <= n <= 10^5 and the array length is n + 1.
Every value in nums is in the range [1, n].
Exactly one value is repeated, but it may appear more than twice.
Solve it without altering the array and with O(1) extra space.
